last_verdict.note: Logic suite green on the desktop (i9-13980HX). +10 vs the start-of-session count from new FIFO/_audio_cb tests + barge-in confirmation tests landed this session. 2 pre-existing numpy divide-by-zero RuntimeWarnings in core/endpointing.py (mel filter), not failures. Whole-tree import smoke also PASS (120 modules).

next: DONE this session (two landings on main): (1) 554b300 -- recovered the PC-interrupted playback rewrite: TTS flows through a callback-driven OutputStream + bounded PlaybackFIFO so the AEC far-end reference is teed from the audio callback at the TRUE playback position (far->near lag now inside DTLN's +/-60ms); barge-in/shutdown flush the FIFO. Adversarial-reviewed (6 minor, 0 blockers); abort/flush contract documented (a true rollback is unsound under concurrent flush/drain), direct _audio_cb tests, pinned notify wake-path. (2) 088e055 -- the interrupt is now 'a bit slower, higher confidence': EchoCoherenceDetector requires coherence_confirm_frames (default 2) CONSECUTIVE over-threshold frames before firing, so a one-off nonlinear-echo spike can't self-interrupt; identity-free (speaker-ID untouched, stays a separate feature). NEXT (P1, needs the mic): AEC on-hardware -- enable aec in config.local.json, calibrate aec_ref_delay_ms + measure post-AEC ERLE with tools/echo_probe.py now that the far-end ref is aligned; live-tune coherence_confirm_frames (2 vs 3) on a real talk-over; then Smart Turn v3 live floor A/B. Latent real-time item in backlog: move coherence note_playback off the audio callback (lock-free SPSC) before raising coherence_ring_ms. (3) AEC CALIBRATION (tools b5ee64a): the FIFO arch fixed the DTLN far-ref sync -- tools.echo_probe ERLE sweep shows cancellation PEAKS at aec_ref_delay_ms=0 (~10-16 dB live) vs 0.8 dB at the old 94ms; built tools/interrupt_suite.py (mic x strategy sweep) + ERLE/override flags in echo_probe. OPEN-SPEAKER BARGE-IN NOW WORKS (self_interruptions=0): config.local.json set to aec dtln ref_delay=0, identity gate OFF (speaker_gate_input=false, interrupt is identity-free), coherence OFF (conflicts with AEC), aec_relaxed_margin_db=6, barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.6. (4) FIRST live test MISSED the real barge -> root cause: _looks_like_user gated the residual vs playback_level (required user LOUDER than the speaker -- wrong post-AEC). FIXED (b33f525): post-AEC barge now gates on loudness_admits(residual vs _ambient_rms) -- echo-only residual ~0.02, real barge ~0.4 (~26dB above floor). Tuned to self_int=0 at input_loudness_margin_db=15 + barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.8. STILL UNVERIFIED (needs a human): that a REAL barge now fires -- run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long answer (should cut off after ~0.8s). The residual math says it WILL fire (0.4 >> ambient+15dB ~0.11). If MISSED: lower min_speech->0.5 / loudness margin->12. (5) ITERATION 3 (e04bb8d): live test had GARBLED TTS (wrong speed/pitch) + still missed barge. FIXED playback rate: device rejects 22050 but PortAudio sometimes 'opened' it anyway -> ran at wrong rate; now probes a supported rate via check_output_settings up front and plays at 44100+resample (portable). FIXED floor-creep: _ambient_rms now FREEZES its rise on bursts (>+6dB) so a sustained talk-over can't raise its own bar (self-calibrating). HONEST CONCLUSION: open ALC285 barge-in is a HARDWARE LIMIT -- the post-AEC residual echo spikes (~0.1-0.3) overlap a normal voice (~0.2-0.4) and vary run-to-run, so a level/sustain gate has NO clean operating point (self_int measured non-monotonic across margins). Best-effort config left (margin 18, sustain 0.8). RELIABLE PATH = HEADPHONES (echo-free -> barge trivially works, snappy; min_speech~0.3, margin~6, aec off). Auto-calibration delivered: playback rate (per-device), AEC delay (FIFO -> ~0), noise floor (freeze-on-burst), coherence margin (EWMA) -- but can't beat the nonlinear-speaker echo. (6) SESSION-ANALYSIS FIXES (9dc9a55): voice fix + barge-fire confirmed live (barge_in_latency~0). Fixed 3 problems from run-20260602-235431: (a) AEC ran on EVERY block incl. no-playback -> DTLN distorted CLEAN speech -> ASR garble; now gated on far-end energy (RMS>1e-4). (b) SenseVoice 2nd-pass hallucinated tiny clips ('SO'->'I then.', 'WHAT'->'I.'); asr_final_min_sec 0.0->0.5 so sub-0.5s clips use the streaming final. (c) barge-in still used speaker-ID via speaker_gate_input; made it IDENTITY-FREE (removed identity from _looks_like_user; speaker-ID gates FINALS only now -- fully decoupled). Not code bugs: TTS queue depth 26 on a long answer = working-as-designed (LLM faster than TTS; barge/stop interrupts it; queue has drop-oldest backpressure); RAM/VRAM ~90% = the gemma3:12b+4b model sizes. 1282 passed. See docs/session_2026-06-02_playback_fifo_aec_alignment.md.
